answer:
The Sentry MCP server is a middleware service that bridges the Model Context Protocol to Sentry’s API, enabling AI agents to access performance data, manage issues, and trigger AI-driven analysis [1][2]. Sentry provides an official remote MCP server hosted on the Sentry platform, which is recommended for most users due to its support for OAuth and superior stability [2][3].

For configuration in clients supporting OAuth, use the URL https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp [2]. For environments requiring local execution, you can use the `npx` command:

```
npx -y mcp-remote@latest https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp
```

Sentry also offers tools to monitor and trace your MCP integrations [4]. While self-hosted or unofficial alternatives exist for specific use cases like private Sentry installations, the official remote server is the primary solution for standard workflows [5][6].

results.title: Yes, Sentry has an MCP Server (...and it’s pretty good) | Sentry Blog
